building_row.notes: The Huxley checks the practical boxes and has live inventory in range, with official and Zillow materials aligning on in-unit laundry, dishwasher, central AC, shared workspace, conference room, rooftop deck, pool, gym, controlled access, and pet-friendliness. The caution is operational texture rather than feature coverage. Public resident signal is mixed: there are positive comments about maintenance and later management improvement, but also complaints about manager turnover, garage theft, and a dorm-like feel. The N La Brea address also makes unit placement critical.

packet_markdown:
# The Huxley building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: the-huxley-1234-n-la-brea-ave
- Address: 1234 N La Brea Ave, West Hollywood, CA 90038
- Primary URL: https://www.essexapartmenthomes.com/apartments/west-hollywood/the-huxley
- Management company: Essex Apartment Homes
- Recommended status: research_complete
- Last updated: 2026-03-30

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 2 (medium, evidence count 1)
- Management: 3 (medium, evidence count 2)
- Amenity: 5 (high, evidence count 3)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 1)
- Location fit: 4
- Pricing transparency: 4

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: unknown
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: medium
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The official Essex page markets a resort-style pool, rooftop deck, fitness center, conference room or shared workspace, floor-to-ceiling windows, spacious storage, and in-home washer and dryer. Source: Essex Apartment Homes official page (https://www.essexapartmenthomes.com/apartments/west-hollywood/the-huxley). Note: Official marketing highlights a resort-style pool, scenic rooftop deck, fitness center, conference room, spacious storage, and in-home washer and dryer.
- [review] Zillow showed live unit-level pricing for 317, 318, and 334 and explicitly said the displayed figures are base rent before mandatory or optional fees. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/west-hollywood-ca/the-huxley/5gjTGR/). Note: Zillow listed Unit 317 at $2,619, Unit 318 at $3,727, and Unit 334 at $3,977, then stated that detailed fee information lives on Essex's Leasing Info page.
- [review] Zillow corroborates controlled access, courtesy patrol, dishwasher, central AC, bike storage, pet park, and EV charging. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/west-hollywood-ca/the-huxley/5gjTGR/). Note: The building amenities and special features sections list controlled access, courtesy patrol, dishwasher, central A/C, bike storage, pet park, and EV charging stations.
- [review] Zillow publishes pet-rent and utility-fee details, but the full recurring monthly stack still requires Essex's leasing sheet. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/west-hollywood-ca/the-huxley/5gjTGR/). Note: The page lists monthly cat rent of $50, dog rent of $75, and a mandatory utility service fee of $3.20 per month, while noting that utilities are otherwise usage-based.
- [review] A public resident review on Zumper described three managers in one year, garage theft, cleanliness issues, and a dorm-like feel. Source: Zumper (https://www.zumper.com/apartment-buildings/p89634/the-huxley-central-hollywood-west-hollywood-ca). Note: The visible review complained about multiple managers in a year, garage theft, and the building feeling like a college dorm.
- [review] Another visible Zumper review said operations improved after a later management change and praised maintenance responsiveness. Source: Zumper (https://www.zumper.com/apartment-buildings/p89634/the-huxley-central-hollywood-west-hollywood-ca). Note: The review said things improved after Dominique arrived and specifically praised David and Roger on maintenance.
- [review] Zillow places The Huxley directly on N La Brea and the marketing copy leans into a high-energy central West Hollywood location, which is convenient but not inherently quiet. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/apartments/west-hollywood-ca/the-huxley/5gjTGR/). Note: The building address is 1234 N La Brea Ave and the copy highlights the heart of West Hollywood with major highways just around the corner.
- [review] The dorm-like resident comment on Zumper suggests a livelier shared-environment risk than the amenity package alone would imply. Source: Zumper (https://www.zumper.com/apartment-buildings/p89634/the-huxley-central-hollywood-west-hollywood-ca). Note: The same negative review explicitly described the building as feeling like a college dorm.

## Open questions
- Only tour units materially buffered from N La Brea, because the location is the biggest quiet tradeoff here.
- Confirm garage security, package handling, and whether courtesy patrol materially reduced prior theft complaints.
- Pull the Essex Leasing Info page and verify the full monthly stack beyond base rent and the visible $3.20 utility service fee.

## Notes
The Huxley checks the practical boxes and has live inventory in range, with official and Zillow materials aligning on in-unit laundry, dishwasher, central AC, shared workspace, conference room, rooftop deck, pool, gym, controlled access, and pet-friendliness. The caution is operational texture rather than feature coverage. Public resident signal is mixed: there are positive comments about maintenance and later management improvement, but also complaints about manager turnover, garage theft, and a dorm-like feel. The N La Brea address also makes unit placement critical.
